content.problemStatement:
Chef received a permutation P1,P2,…,PNP_1, P_2, \ldots, P_NP1​,P2​,…,PN​ and also an integer DDD from his good friend Grux, because Grux was afraid he would forget them somewhere. However, since Grux was just playing with the permutation, it was all shuffled, and Chef only likes sorted permutations, so he decided to sort it by performing some swaps.

Chef wants to use the integer DDD he just received, so he is only willing to swap two elements of the permutation whenever their absolute difference is exactly DDD. He has limited time, so you should determine the minimum number of swaps he needs to perform to sort the permutation, or tell him that it is impossible to sort it his way.
constraints: 1≤Pi≤N1 \le P_i \le N1≤Pi​≤N for each valid iii
P1,P2,…,PNP_1, P_2, \ldots, P_NP1​,P2​,…,PN​ are pairwise distinct
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10,00010,00010,000
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and DDD.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers P1,P2,…,PNP_1, P_2, \ldots, P_NP1​,P2​,…,PN​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ― the minimum number of swaps, or −1-1−1 if it is impossible to sort the permutation.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: Chef can perform the following swaps in this order:

swap the first and fifth element
swap the third and fifth element
swap the second and fourth element
